skill.name: azure-communication-sms-java
skill.description: Guides Java developers through Azure Communication Services SMS setup, client creation, message sending, delivery reports, and error handling.
skill.summary: Build SMS sending workflows in Java with Azure Communication Services.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences as shell backtick execution and placeholder Azure URLs as network usage. Review found documentation examples for the Azure Communication Services SMS Java SDK, with no prompt injection, malware intent, or executable automation. The remaining risk is low because the skill discusses SMS delivery and credential placeholders that users must configure safely.

low_findings.title: Markdown Fences Misclassified as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to Markdown fenced examples, not Ruby or shell command execution. The snippets show XML, Java, Bash-style environment variable examples, and Markdown tables for SDK usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown code fences and examples. No instruction executes commands or passes user input to a shell.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Azure Endpoint and Credential Examples
low_findings.description: The network findings are legitimate Azure Communication Services endpoint placeholders. The skill does not send data itself, but users will configure real service endpoints and credentials when using the SDK.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are documented Azure placeholders, and the connection string line uses placeholder values. This is expected for an SMS SDK guide.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Findings Not Supported
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance alerts are not supported by the reviewed text. The cited sections describe SMS metadata, delivery status, and HTTP response handling, not cryptography or host reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content contains SMS descriptions, result properties, and delivery report fields. No cryptographic operation or host discovery behavior is present.

content.user_title: Build Azure SMS Workflows in Java
content.value_statement: Java teams need reliable SMS notifications without guessing Azure SDK patterns. This skill provides focused guidance for sending, tracking, and handling SMS messages with Azure Communication Services.
actual_capabilities: Shows Maven dependency setup for the Azure Communication Services SMS Java package.
Explains client creation with DefaultAzureCredential, connection strings, and AzureKeyCredential.
Provides examples for single-recipient and multi-recipient SMS sends.
Covers delivery report enablement and Event Grid report handling concepts.
Documents response handling, per-recipient errors, and common retry considerations.
limitations: Does not provision Azure Communication Services resources or phone numbers.
Does not validate phone number ownership, consent, or regional messaging compliance.
Does not include a complete runnable application or test suite.
Uses placeholder credentials and endpoints that must be replaced securely.

use_cases.title: Add OTP Delivery
use_cases.description: Implement one-time passcode SMS delivery with result checking and clear error handling.

use_cases.title: Send Operational Alerts
use_cases.description: Create Java SMS alerting for incidents, outages, and high-priority system events.

use_cases.title: Track Bulk Notifications
use_cases.description: Send messages to multiple recipients and correlate delivery outcomes with business tags.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up the SDK
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the Maven dependency and safest client setup for Azure Communication Services SMS in Java.

prompt_templates.title: Send One Message
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me send one SMS from a Java service and check whether the send succeeded.

prompt_templates.title: Add Delivery Tracking
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Java SMS workflow with delivery reports, tags, and per-recipient error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Review Production Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Azure SMS Java design for retries, credential handling, compliance, and delivery observability.

output_examples.input: I need to send an OTP SMS from a Java backend.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan covering Azure client setup, sender number configuration, message sending, result checks, and safe secret storage.

output_examples.input: I want delivery reports for bulk SMS messages.
output_examples.output: Guidance for enabling delivery reports, assigning tracking tags, receiving Event Grid events, and correlating statuses with recipients.

output_examples.input: My SMS requests sometimes fail with rate limits.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting checklist for status handling, per-recipient failures, retry backoff, and operational logging.

best_practices: Use managed identity or secure secret storage instead of hardcoded connection strings.
Use E.164 phone number format and validate consent before sending messages.
Enable delivery reports for important workflows and log message identifiers securely.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode Azure access keys, connection strings, or recipient lists in source code.
Do not send marketing or bulk messages without consent and regional compliance checks.
Do not treat request success as delivery success without checking delivery reports when needed.

faq.question: Does this skill send SMS messages by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides Java SDK guidance and examples. Your application sends messages through Azure Communication Services.

faq.question: Which authentication methods are covered?
faq.answer: It covers DefaultAzureCredential, connection strings, and AzureKeyCredential patterns for creating SMS clients.

faq.question: Can it help with bulk SMS?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes multi-recipient send patterns and guidance for handling each recipient result.

faq.question: Does it cover delivery reports?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains enabling delivery reports and using Event Grid to receive delivery status events.

faq.question: Does it include compliance guidance?
faq.answer: Only basic operational guidance. You must verify consent, opt-out rules, and regional SMS regulations separately.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file is documentation-only, with low risk from service endpoint and credential configuration examples.
